---
title: "TidyTuesday - week 23"
author: "Maja Urankar"
date: "2026-05-31"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)
library(lubridate)
library(ggtext)
```

```{r}
game_films <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-06-09/game_films.csv')
```
```{r}
highlight_title <- "Final Fantasy VII: Advent Children"

game_films %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = rotten_tomatoes, y = metacritic)) + 
  geom_point(color = "grey65", alpha = 0.45, size = 2.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.7, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_point(data = ~ filter(.x, title == highlight_title), color = "#D55E00", size = 4) +
  geom_text_repel(data = ~ filter(.x, title == highlight_title), aes(label = title), color = "#D55E00", 
                  size = 4.5, nudge_y = 6, nudge_x = 7, segment.color = "#D55E00", segment.linewidth = 0.4, box.padding = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Critics agree… except when they don’t",
    x = "Rotten Tomatoes critic score",
    y = "Metacritic score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 22),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, color = "grey30", lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "grey45", hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey88"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.margin = margin(15, 20, 15, 15)
  )
```
